items.prompt: In KL, D(v) is defined as…
After a KL pass you should keep the entire swap sequence even if cumulative gain peaked earlier.
items.explain: Rollback to the best prefix; late swaps can hurt.

items.prompt: On the course seed partition, cutsize before KL is…
items.explain: The seed cuts A–B and D–E (among others) for cut 12.

items.prompt: The reference KL run accepts which first improving swap?
items.explain: Swap (A, D) with gain 9 reduces cut 12 → 3.

items.prompt: KL is a refinement method: it needs an initial bipartition to improve.
items.explain: KL does not create a partition from scratch.
